diagnosis: The strategy implements a full Ichimoku cloud breakout system with crypto-adapted parameters (tenkan=10, kijun=34, senkou_b=59, displacement=30). It computes Tenkan-sen, Kijun-sen, Senkou Span A, and Senkou Span B internally via rolling Donchian midlines. Entry logic: detects a bar where close crosses above the cloud top (bullish_breakout) or below the cloud bottom (bearish_breakout), optionally confirmed by Chikou span and/or a concurrent TK cross. CRITICAL: the strategy DOES emit short signals — line 138 sets side = "short" if bearish_breakout. The short branch is fully live, with inverted stop/TP arithmetic (lines 151-153). In evolved PARAMS, require_chikou_confirm=False, require_tk_cross=True (confusingly, code logic at lines 132-135 means TK cross is required for longs AND a bearish TK cross for shorts). ADX filter at 12.1 is weak — nearly always passes. Regime gate: only ["trend","breakout"]. Walk-forward reported Sharpe 2.28, PF 1.30 over 106 trades, but this was with the short branch live. Core edge for longs: cloud breakouts in crypto trend strongly — the cloud acts as a thick dynamic S/R zone and breakouts above a wide cloud represent genuine momentum continuation, not noise. Weakness: pure breakout (no mean-reversion awareness), high latency in components (30-bar displacement means cloud lags badly during fast reversals), and the internal loop re-computes Donchian midlines from scratch on every bar (O(n*period) per bar, but not a correctness issue).
long_only_hypothesis: In crypto spot markets, upward Kumo breakouts reliably precede sustained trending moves: the cloud condenses months of price action into a single dynamic support zone, so a confirmed close above the cloud top (especially when the cloud is thick, ADX is rising, and volume is expanding) identifies the transition from range/distribution to markup phase. The hypothesis is: enter long when close breaks above cloud top with concurrent TK alignment and regime = trend/breakout; stay in cash otherwise, including when the bearish breakout would have triggered a short. The bullish-cloud scenario aligns with the crypto risk-on cycle that dominates spot returns 2019-2026; longs capture the majority of profitable Ichimoku signals historically. The short signals are dropped entirely — any bearish breakout becomes a flat/cash signal, meaning the strategy exits any open long (via signal_exit) and does not re-enter until a new bullish breakout forms.
market_techniques: Kumo (cloud) breakout momentum — enter long only on confirmed close above cloud top, drop short branch entirely
Senkou Span B as dynamic trailing stop floor — update hard stop to cloud_top - buffer after entry rather than fixed ATR multiple
TK cross as entry quality filter — require tenkan > kijun at entry (momentum confirmation)
Cloud thickness as position-sizing signal — thicker cloud = stronger structure break = larger size fraction
Regime gating — restrict entries to trend/breakout regimes, add explicit bear-market gate via BMSB or BTC drawdown filter
ADX trend-strength filter — raise ADX threshold above the current 12 to require genuine trending conditions
Volume confirmation at breakout — volume_ratio spike validates institutional participation at the cloud break
Chikou span as secondary confirmation — price above its own displaced value confirms broader trend alignment
Time stop as position duration cap — 72h cap prevents trades from degenerating into open-ended drawdowns in ranging conditions
ATR-based stop below cloud bottom — stop anchored to cloud_bottom - 0.5*ATR is structurally motivated rather than arbitrary
useful_features: trend: adx_14 — primary trend-strength gate, raise min threshold
trend: roc_12 — rate-of-change confirms momentum direction at breakout bar
trend: bmsb_bullish — bull-market support band as macro long-side gate, avoids entries in secular bear
volatility: atr_14 — stop placement and position sizing denominator
volatility: bb_bw_percentile — cloud breakouts after volatility compression (low percentile) are higher-quality
volatility: gk_vol — Garman-Klass vol for volatility targeting position sizing
volume: volume_ratio — confirms breakout with above-average volume (require_volume gate)
volume: volume_zscore — spike detection at breakout bar
volume: obv_divergence — OBV trending up before price breakout is leading confirmation
momentum: rsi_14 — avoid overbought entries (rsi > 75 after breakout often means chasing late), use as exit signal
momentum: ret_zscore_20 — z-score of recent returns, high positive z at entry warns of extended move
momentum: price_acceleration — second derivative of price, positive acceleration at cloud break is bullish
market_regime: regime parameter passed to strategy — already used, but add BTC/ETH drawdown-from-ATH as informative feature
market_regime: dist_from_high_20 — close near 20-bar high at breakout confirms strength not exhaustion
mean_reversion: bb_pct_b — secondary awareness that price is not already at extreme upper band before entry
microstructure: pressure_imbalance — buying pressure > selling pressure at breakout bar confirms demand
microstructure: close_location — high close_location (close near high of bar) on breakout bar is bullish confirmation

genome_params.name: tenkan_period
genome_params.note: Faster Tenkan captures crypto momentum; tight range around evolved value of 10

genome_params.name: kijun_period
genome_params.note: Kijun defines medium-term equilibrium; wider than tenkan by design

genome_params.name: senkou_b_period
genome_params.note: Long cloud boundary; should stay ~2x kijun to maintain structural validity

genome_params.name: displacement
genome_params.note: Cloud forward projection lag; tied to kijun_period conceptually

genome_params.name: adx_min
genome_params.note: Raise floor from evolved 12.1 to require genuine trend; upper bound avoids filtering too many valid entries

genome_params.name: stop_loss_atr_mult
genome_params.note: ATR stop multiplier; lower bound gives tighter stops that suit confirmed breakouts

genome_params.name: take_profit_atr_mult
genome_params.note: TP multiplier; wide TP suits trending regime expectation

genome_params.name: require_chikou_confirm
genome_params.note: Toggle Chikou span confirmation; can reduce false breakouts but reduces trade count

genome_params.name: require_tk_cross
genome_params.note: Toggle concurrent TK cross; current PARAMS has True

genome_params.name: require_volume
genome_params.note: Volume spike confirmation toggle; default off but worth sweeping

genome_params.name: volume_threshold
genome_params.note: Only active when require_volume=True; 1.2-1.5 typical for genuine breakout confirmation

genome_params.name: time_stop_hours
genome_params.note: Trade duration cap; sweep to find optimal holding window for long-only regime

do_not_optimize: allowed_regimes — hardcoded to [trend, breakout] for long-only; changing the set has only 3-4 meaningful combinations, which will massively overfit to specific OOS periods
require_trend_regime — should be locked True for long-only spot; toggling it off removes the regime gate entirely and invites whipsaws
displacement — tightly coupled to kijun_period by Ichimoku theory (should equal kijun); if kijun is swept displacement should track it (kijun_period value), not be independently optimized
senkou_b_period — should remain approximately 2x kijun_period; independent sweeping breaks the structural relationship and produces non-Ichimoku hybrids that are curve-fit artifacts

risk_management.stop: ATR hard stop below cloud_bottom - 0.5*atr_14 at entry (structurally motivated); fall back to close - stop_loss_atr_mult * atr_14 if cloud_bottom is above entry (rare case after fast move); minimum stop distance = 1.5 * atr_14
risk_management.take_profit: Fixed take_profit = close + take_profit_atr_mult * atr_14 at entry; evolved value 6.82x ATR suits trending regimes; sweep 3.0-8.0x
risk_management.trailing: Optional trailing stop: once unrealized gain > 2*atr_14, raise stop to cloud_top (current displaced value) — this locks in cloud structure as a trailing floor and is conceptually grounded in Ichimoku theory
risk_management.vol_target: Volatility-targeted sizing using gk_vol: target 20% annualized portfolio vol; size = equity * (vol_target / current_annual_vol); cap at max_position_pct; this naturally reduces size during high-vol regimes
risk_management.max_size: Maximum 40% of equity per trade (config.max_position_pct); cloud breakouts can fail fast so over-concentration is dangerous; strength scalar 0.6-1.0 from current code already modulates this
risk_management.cooldown: Minimum 12h cooldown after any stop_loss exit; prevent re-entry into the same failed breakout bar; implement via time check in strategy_fn against last exit timestamp
risk_management.risk_off: Block all new longs when: (1) bmsb_bullish == False for 3 consecutive days (macro bear gate), (2) dist_from_high_20 < -0.15 on weekly timeframe equivalent (deep drawdown in recent history), (3) regime not in [trend, breakout] (already implemented), (4) adx_14 < adx_min (already implemented)

metrics: CAGR (annualized) — primary return target; expect 25-60% for long-only crypto trend strategy
Sharpe ratio (annualized, risk-free 0%) — target > 1.0; walk-forward reported 2.28 with shorts, long-only will likely be lower
Sortino ratio — penalizes only downside vol; more appropriate for long-only crypto with asymmetric return distribution
Maximum drawdown (%) — critical for spot long-only; cloud strategies can sit in cash for extended periods, so max DD should be better than buy-and-hold
Calmar ratio (CAGR / maxDD) — composite risk-adjusted metric; target > 0.5
Hit rate (% winning trades) — with wide TP and tight SL, expect 35-50%; high profit factor compensates low hit rate
Profit factor (gross profit / gross loss) — target > 1.4; current PARAMS has 1.30 with shorts included
Trade count and turnover — 106 trades over full history is ~15-20 per year; adequate for statistical significance; monitor if long-only filtering halves this
Average trade duration (hours) — with 72h time stop; distinguish TP exits (short duration, good) from time stops (may indicate failed breakouts)
Market exposure (% of time in a long position) — critical for long-only; higher exposure means more beta-like returns
Cost sensitivity — re-run at 2x and 5x commission/slippage assumptions; Ichimoku breakouts are infrequent enough that costs should be manageable
Bootstrap Sharpe confidence interval (1000 resample) — verify Sharpe > 0.8 at 5th percentile
Walk-forward per-fold consistency — Sharpe should be positive in at least 6/8 folds on pre-2024 history; one negative fold is acceptable
Per-year return stability 2019-2023 — check that no single year (especially 2021 bull or 2022 bear) dominates cumulative PnL
sweep_changes: REMOVE the short branch: delete lines 113 (bearish_breakout detection), 115 (bearish_breakout in compound condition), the short-side logic in Chikou confirm block (lines 126-127), short-side TK check (lines 135-136), and lines 151-153 (short stop/TP arithmetic); replace with: if not bullish_breakout: return None
FIX the side assignment line 138: remove the ternary, always set side = 'long'
ADD bmsb_bullish macro gate: before bullish_breakout check, if current.get('bmsb_bullish', True) == False: return None (long-only risk-off macro filter)
RAISE adx_min floor in param_space from (10.0, 25.0) to (15.0, 30.0) — the evolved 12.1 is too permissive; a genuine trend filter requires at least 15

ADD cloud_bottom trailing stop update logic: after 2*atr_14 unrealized gain, update position.stop_loss to max(position.stop_loss, cloud_bottom_current) inside the position management block (requires passing cloud values through metadata or recomputing on each bar)
ADD volume_threshold sweep to param_space: currently fixed at 0.8 when require_volume=True; sweep (0.8, 2.0) as float
ADD time_stop_hours to param_space with range (48, 120) as int — currently hardcoded at 72, which is not swept
ADD cooldown enforcement: track last_stop_loss_exit_time in strategy state or via metadata; skip entry if current_timestamp - last_stop_time < cooldown_hours * 3600000
ADD strength calculation enhancement: incorporate volume_zscore into strength scalar (volume_zscore > 1.5 adds 0.1 to base 0.6) so position sizing naturally reflects breakout quality
UPDATE allowed_regimes to lock to ['trend', 'breakout'] only (already default); remove it from param_space to prevent it from being independently toggled during genome evolution (overfitting risk)
methodological_risks: Short-branch contamination of walk-forward metrics: the reported WF Sharpe=2.28 includes short trades; long-only backtest will have materially different (likely lower) statistics and cannot be compared directly to the existing benchmark

Displacement lag creates look-ahead ambiguity: the code uses senkou_a/b at index [last - disp] to represent the 'current' cloud, but classic Ichimoku displaces the cloud FORWARD by 26 bars, meaning the current bar's cloud was computed disp bars ago — the implementation is correct but easy to misread; verify the cloud values align with TradingView reference

Per-bar Donchian recomputation: the inner loop in _donchian_mid recomputes midlines from scratch on every strategy call; this is O(n*period) per call but produces correct results; for production sweeps with many param configurations this is slow, consider pre-computing in features.py

ADX computed in features.py uses EMA-smoothed DM (not Wilder smoothing); this differs from standard ADX definition and will produce different crossover levels — the adx_min threshold calibrated against this non-standard ADX cannot be directly compared to published Ichimoku ADX thresholds

Cloud thickness filter uses a 1% absolute threshold (cloud_thickness > 1.0 adds 0.15 strength); this threshold is not evolved and may be too tight for early history (2019-2020) where BTC price was much lower and ATR% was higher, or too loose in mature 2025-2026 market

Regime label dependency: the regime parameter is passed externally; if the regime classifier has lookahead or regime changes are detected with a lag, the regime gate may inadvertently filter good trades or pass bad ones — validate regime classifier independence

Low trade count risk: dropping the short branch may reduce 106 historical trades to ~40-60 long-only trades; this is marginal for statistical significance testing; bootstrap CI will be wide and Sharpe estimates unreliable

Parameter space collapse between senkou_b_period and displacement: if both are independently swept, the optimizer will find pathological combinations (e.g., displacement > kijun_period) that violate Ichimoku theory and overfit to specific market structures

OOS lockbox integrity: the evolved PARAMS (recorded 2026-03-22) fall within the 2-year OOS window (2024-06-07 to 2026-06-07), meaning the current best-evolved params were optimized on data that includes OOS period; any final OOS evaluation with these params is compromised and must use a clean re-optimization on pre-2024 history only

BMSB macro gate calibration risk: bmsb_sma/ema periods in features.py are in bar units (20/21 bars for hourly data = ~20 hours, not 20 weeks); if the feature was intended to represent weekly SMAs, the current parameterization is orders of magnitude too short and the bmsb_bullish signal is essentially noise at the hourly timeframe
